parameters.text:
=Transform this book chapter into a short-form dialogue between two characters for a {{ $('Globals1').item.json.NICHE_NAME }} YouTube Shorts channel.

=== CHARACTERS ===
{{ $('Globals1').item.json.CHARACTER_A_NAME }} (A): {{ $('Globals1').item.json.CHARACTER_A_PERSONALITY }}
{{ $('Globals1').item.json.CHARACTER_B_NAME }} (B): {{ $('Globals1').item.json.CHARACTER_B_PERSONALITY }}

=== AUDIENCE ===
{{ $('Globals1').item.json.NICHE_AUDIENCE }}

=== SOURCE CHAPTER ===
Book: {{ $json.book_title }} — by {{ $json.book_author }}
Chapter: {{ $json.chapter_number }}. {{ $json.chapter_title }}

{{ $json.chapter_text }}

=== ANGLES TO AVOID (already rejected) ===
{{ JSON.stringify($json.avoid_angles) }}

=== YOUR PROCESS ===
STEP 1 (internal, do not output): Brainstorm 3 angles on this chapter:
  a) wait-what (counter-intuitive)
  b) your-life (connects to daily experience)
  c) reframe (flips a common assumption)
Score each on hook strength, relatability, shareability, curiosity gap, and distinctness from avoid list.

STEP 2: Pick the highest combined score. Write the dialogue.

=== DIALOGUE RULES ===
- Exactly 4 to 7 lines total, alternating speakers, starting with A.
- Total spoken length: 55-75 words across all lines combined.
- First line is the HOOK (6-12 words, from A). It must use a proven pattern: contradiction, personal callout, specific number, future shock, pattern interrupt.
- Never start with: 'Did you know', 'Have you ever', 'Imagine if', 'Scientists discovered', 'A new study'.
- Never mention the book, the author, or that this is a summary.
- A voices doubt/resistance/the dumb-sounding question. B voices the insight.
- B never lectures. Lines are conversational, not didactic.
- Final line lands the thought. No CTA.
- Each line is 1-2 sentences max. Short. Punchy. Reads aloud like real speech.
- 8th grade reading level. No 'furthermore', 'essentially', 'in conclusion'.
- Banned words: fascinating, incredible, mind-blowing, game-changer, literally (as filler), unhinged, low-key, high-key.

=== ON-SCREEN CAPTION ===
A 4-8 word bold text shown in the first 1.5s. Different wording from the spoken hook. Triggers curiosity.

=== OUTPUT ===
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown fences):

{
  "selectedAngle": "wait-what | your-life | reframe",
  "title": "YouTube Shorts title, ≤60 chars, includes a searchable keyword",
  "oneLineSummary": "12-18 word neutral description of the core insight",
  "viralScore": 1-10,
  "onScreenCaption": "4-8 word bold opening text",
  "keywords": ["3-5 searchable terms"],
  "lines": [
    { "speaker": "A", "text": "the spoken line" }
  ]
}

options.systemMessage: You write punchy two-character dialogue for short-form video. Your scripts feel like overheard conversation between a doubter and an explainer — never like a podcast, never like a lecture. Output strict JSON only, no preamble or markdown fences.
Content librarian. Strict JSON output only.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ text: $json.text, model_id: $('Globals1').item.json.ELEVENLABS_MODEL_ID, voice_settings: { stability: 0.4, similarity_boost: 0.8, style: 0.15, use_speaker_boost: true }, output_format: 'mp3_44100_128' }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({
  template_id: $('Globals1').item.json.CREATOMATE_TEMPLATE_ID,
  output_format: 'mp4',
  frame_rate: 30,
  modifications: {
    'Background.source': $json.bg_public_url,
    'Audio.source': $json.audio_public_url,
    'Music.source': $('Globals1').item.json.MUSIC_BED_URL,
    'SpriteA.source': $('Globals1').item.json.CHARACTER_A_SPRITE_URL,
    'SpriteB.source': $('Globals1').item.json.CHARACTER_B_SPRITE_URL,
    'SpriteA.animations': $json.sprite_a_windows.map(w => ({ time: w.start, duration: w.end - w.start, type: 'fade', easing: 'linear', transition: false, value_from: 1, value_to: 1 })),
    'SpriteB.animations': $json.sprite_b_windows.map(w => ({ time: w.start, duration: w.end - w.start, type: 'fade', easing: 'linear', transition: false, value_from: 1, value_to: 1 })),
    'Hook.text': $json.idea_on_screen_caption,
    'Captions.transcript_source': $json.audio_public_url,
    'Captions.transcript_effect': 'highlight',
    'Captions.transcript_color': '#FFFFFF',
    'Title.text': $json.idea_title
  }
}) }}

options.systemMessage: You write Shorts metadata that ranks. Strict JSON only.

parameters.text:
=✅ Scheduled *{{ $('Compute Publish Time').item.json.yt_title }}* for {{ $('Compute Publish Time').item.json.publish_at_iso }}
{{ 'https://youtube.com/shorts/' + $('Upload to YouTube').item.json.id }}

parameters.content:
## Workflow B — Daily Shorts (Dialogue + Minecraft)

**Schedule:** runs at 07:00 daily, generates `VIDEOS_PER_RUN` (default 3) shorts.

**Flow:** Cron → pick chapter → script as two-character dialogue → ElevenLabs per line → FFmpeg concat → random Minecraft clip from Drive → FFmpeg trim+crop to 9:16 → Creatomate assembly with sprite swap windows → Telegram preview → approval → YouTube schedule.

**Required on the n8n host:**
- FFmpeg installed and in PATH (`apt-get install ffmpeg` on Debian/Ubuntu)
- Write access to `FFMPEG_WORK_DIR` (default `/tmp/n8n-shorts`)
- The `Execute Command` node must be enabled (it is on standard self-hosted n8n)

**Required Sheets/Drive:**
- `book_manifest`, `videos_log`, `errors_log` sheets (same schema as Workflow A's notes)
- A Drive folder full of Minecraft parkour MP4s, each at least 60 seconds long
- A public folder for audio handoff to Creatomate
- A public folder for background-clip handoff to Creatomate
- Both character sprites hosted at public URLs (Drive public links work)

## Creatomate Template Spec

Build a 720x1280 (9:16) template with these element NAMES exactly:

- `Background` — video element, full frame, source set per-render
- `Audio` — audio element, source set per-render (the merged narration)
- `Music` — audio element, source set per-render, volume ~15%
- `SpriteA` — image element, transparent PNG, positioned lower-left ~30% width, opacity controlled by `animations` array sent per-render (windows where A is talking, opacity 1; otherwise 0)
- `SpriteB` — image element, lower-right ~30% width, same animation pattern
- `Hook` — large bold text, top-third, shown 0–1.5s only
- `Captions` — animated word-level captions sourced from the Audio element's transcript, centered, highlight effect, bold, large stroke
- `Title` — small text, top, channel branding or video title

The sprite opacity animation pattern: set the element's base opacity to 0, then pass an `animations` array with per-window `{ time, duration, value_from: 1, value_to: 1 }` entries. Outside those windows the element stays at base opacity (0).

If Creatomate's API rejects this exact animation shape, alternative: use `keyframes` on opacity property with `{time, value}` pairs derived from the windows.

assignments.name: CHARACTER_A_PERSONALITY
assignments.value: The skeptical everyman. Asks the dumb-sounding question that turns out to be the right one. Mildly cynical, dry, drops the occasional self-deprecating jab. Voices doubt, resistance, and what the audience is secretly thinking.

assignments.name: CHARACTER_B_PERSONALITY
assignments.value: The patient explainer with a slight edge. Knows the answer, delivers it precisely, occasionally cutting when Max says something especially wrong. Never lectures, never moralizes — just lands the insight and lets it sit.

assignments.name: MUSIC_BED_URL
assignments.value: <__PLACEHOLDER__Public URL to royalty-free music bed MP3, looped if needed__>
